summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special zoning workshop with the Zoning Commission, Board of Zoning Appeals, and attorney Kevin Mayloff to discuss tools for managing residential development. Key topics included New Community Authorities (NCAs) as a mechanism to assess taxes on new subdivisions to offset community impacts, potential increases to minimum residential lot sizes, setback requirement changes, a possible moratorium on Residential Planned District (RPD) applications, and the need for a comprehensive land use plan. Trustee Hauserman was absent, so major decisions were deferred to a future meeting.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed (Allen: Yes, Hodges: Yes)
Motion to adjourn the special meeting at 7:18 PM passed (Allen: Yes, Hodges: Yes)
action_items: John Langenheim to research legality and process for implementing a moratorium on RPD/PD applications
John Langenheim to develop verbiage for changing PUD side setback requirements and two-acre lot corridor east of Goshen Road
Kevin Mayloff to provide list of NCA success stories and connect with attorney Emmett Kelly at Brown Todd Columbus office for further education
John Langenheim to obtain sewer and water boundary maps from Clermont County Water and Sewer and Western Water Services
John Langenheim to follow up with John McManus at Clermont County Water and Soil on stream/water setback requirement text
Trustees to reach out to attorney Ben Yoder regarding moratorium guidance
John Langenheim to forward Ohio Bar Association article on NCAs tied to TIFs/CRAs to trustees
Schedule next special meeting soon with all three trustees present to continue discussions
David Dinwoody suggested Zoning Commission schedule special meetings to expedite verbiage and voting on zoning changes
key_amounts: $50,000 estimated cost for a septic system on a two-acre parcel
$66,000 per lot estimated cost for sewer and road infrastructure in a 25-lot development
$250,000 estimated cost to bring natural gas down Smith Road (cited by Duke Energy)
$150,000 potential cost per two-acre lot to a developer
$80,000 potential lot cost if NCA is used to spread infrastructure costs over 30 years
$200,000 potential cost per two-acre lot without NCA assistance
$250,000-$500,000 estimated cost to bring sewer to a development site
notable_topics: New Community Authority (NCA) under ORC 349 as a tool to assess taxes on new subdivisions
Minimum residential lot size increases (two-acre and five-acre lot discussions)
Residential single-family setback requirement changes (particularly side setbacks from 10 to 15-20 feet)
Potential moratorium on Residential Planned District (RPD/PD) applications
Opposition to TIFs and CRAs for residential development
Need for a comprehensive land use plan for the township
Sewer and water expansion concerns and their impact on development pressure
Township identity discussion: rural vs. suburban vs. urban
Stream/waterway setback requirements proposed by Clermont County Water and Soil
Balancing farmer property rights with community growth management
attendees: Alyssa (BZA or ZC member, mentioned but did not speak)
notes: Trustee Hauserman was hospitalized the prior week and absent from the meeting; major decisions were deferred until he can participate. Trustee Allen strongly expressed opposition to TIFs and CRAs for residential development. Steve Pegram (Zoning Commission) emphasized that over 60% of the township is zoned R1 rural residential agricultural and raised concern about the township's identity crisis regarding rural/suburban/urban classification. Lewis from the audience noted Hamilton Township lost a lawsuit approximately five years ago when they attempted a moratorium on PUDs. The meeting referenced recent severe weather (nasty weekend/tornadoes). Kevin Mayloff referenced his personal development experience with Stone Pillars and Klosterman Farm preservation. The 2017 elimination of agricultural base layer zoning was noted.
title: BOT Special Zoning Workshop 4-3-25
